message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Children of Immigrants Longitudinal Survey in Four European Countries (CILS4EU) – Germany, Wave 1-3, 2010-2013","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/cils4eu.5653.3.3.0","justification":"Longitudinal survey of children of immigrants in Germany (part of a four-country study), waves 2010–2013. Focuses on youth with migration background, education trajectories (including transition to higher education), family, social behaviour and attitudes. Covers Germany in the early 2010s."},{"title":"German Youth Institute (DJI) Youth Survey 2010: Growing up in Germany","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5672","justification":"Survey of young people in Germany around 2010 examining education, training, leisure, family life, attitudes and social behaviour. Focus on youth and adolescents; includes questions on school and further education, social integration and leisure activities."},{"title":"German Youth Institute (DJI) Youth Survey 2015: Growing up in Germany","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA6716","justification":"Follow-up youth survey in Germany mid‑2010s including education, training (vocational and further education), leisure, family relations and social attitudes. Focuses on young people’s life situations in Germany."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP) – Survey Period 2010-2019 (GESIS Edition extracts)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5274","justification":"Longitudinal household survey for Germany throughout the 2010s with rich information on education (including higher education), family status, children, migration background, social attitudes and leisure. While not youth‑only, it includes extensive youth and children modules and is widely used for research on education and family in Germany."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Family and Changing Gender Roles IV – Germany 2012","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5900","justification":"German part of ISSP 2012 with focus on family life, marriage, gender roles and children. Contains social attitudes towards family and work–family arrangements in Germany in the 2010s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Leisure Time and Sports – Germany 2011","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5560","justification":"German module of the ISSP on leisure time and sports (2011). Focuses on participation in leisure and sports activities and related attitudes in Germany during the 2010s; relevant for social behaviour, leisure, tourism and sport."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 2010","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA4600","justification":"Representative survey of the adult population in Germany in 2010 covering social structure, education, family status, attitudes and social behaviour. Includes information on education levels, family life and some leisure-related topics."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 2012 – Special Topic: Religion","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA4610","justification":"ALLBUS wave for Germany 2012 with general modules on education, family, social structure and attitudes plus special topic religion. Although not youth-specific, it covers adults in Germany and includes variables on education and family circumstances in the 2010s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 2014 – Special Topic: Social Inequality","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5045","justification":"ALLBUS 2014 focuses on perceptions of social inequality and mobility, including education and occupational status, and includes questions on family structure and attitudes. Provides Germany-wide data on adults in mid‑2010s."},{"title":"Panel Study ‘Labour Market and Social Security’ (PASS) – Waves 2010-2019 (GESIS documentation)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5278","justification":"Longitudinal panel of individuals and households in Germany receiving or at risk of receiving unemployment benefits. Contains detailed information on education (including further and vocational education), family composition, migration background and social participation during the 2010s."}]}
